name: Angle Explorer
description:
Angle Explorer is an arcade-style maths game for learning angles through an animated cannon mechanic.

WHAT IT DOES:
A cannon rotates from a fixed base. Drag it to aim, then fire at the target egg. As it rotates the live angle value updates and snaps to key positions.

WHAT IT TEACHES:
- Level 1: Recognising and targeting acute, right, obtuse, straight and reflex angles by sight — drag the cannon to the correct angle and fire
- Level 2: Missing angles — given a set of sectors that sum to 90°, 180°, or 360°, find the unknown angle and fire
- Level 3 (coming soon): Angle reasoning without visual hints — pure calculation questions

INTERACTION MODEL:
Drag the cannon barrel to aim, type the angle on the keypad, then fire. Correct shots collect stars. Collect enough to trigger a Monster Round — faster, tougher questions for bonus stars.

PEDAGOGICAL NOTES:
- Level 1 builds physical intuition by coupling the drag gesture directly to the angle value — students feel the difference between acute, right, obtuse and reflex before they name them
- Snapping to 90° and 180° reinforces the landmark totals that underpin complementary and supplementary reasoning
- Level 2 introduces missing-angle problems through sector diagrams: students must identify which part is unknown and subtract from the total, targeting the common error of adding all visible parts instead
- The Monster Round raises stakes without changing the concept — same maths, faster pace, rewarding fluency over guessing
- Level 3 (coming soon) removes all visual scaffolding, moving students from diagram-supported reasoning to pure symbolic calculation

TECH: React, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, SVG.
